title: Informational Interviews and Reality Testing · Hiring Signal Forensics
summary: A realistic informational interviews and reality testing artifact contains one plausible targeting, evidence, integrity, access, privacy, process, or decision defect. Locate the first unsupported claim before revising it. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in consented role-reality interview study and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define a concrete uncertainty about tasks, environment, entry routes, evidence, or tradeoffs that a practitioner can help test.
Research public context and explain why this person may have relevant perspective without presuming identity, availability, or representation.
Plan a short accessible conversation with neutral behavior questions, time consent, recording consent, and an easy stopping path.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Learning Question
steps.instruction:
debug case — A nominal learning conversation hides a referral request, asks generic questions, records without consent, and generalizes one practitioner view to a whole market. The debug variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Define a concrete uncertainty about tasks, environment, entry routes, evidence, or tradeoffs that a practitioner can help test.

Inspect the Learning Question trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Learning Question.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define a concrete uncertainty about tasks, environment, entry routes, evidence, or tradeoffs that a practitioner can help test. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Learning Question, keep Learning Question connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: For Learning Question, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Inspect the failure record · Debug Learning Question, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Learning Question precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Inspect the failure record · Debug Learning Question, trace this concrete input and predict targeting, source, evidence, contribution, access, privacy, consent, process, decision, rejection, repair, follow-up, and learner-controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A nominal learning conversation hides a referral request, asks generic questions, records without consent, and generalizes one practitioner view to a whole market. The debug variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Define a concrete uncertainty about tasks, environment, entry routes, evidence, or tradeoffs that a practitioner can help test.

For Inspect the failure record · Debug Learning Question, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: The goal of an informational interview is to impress the contact.
